codeReview.summary: The hard-cut correctly threads supplier settlements and auto-settle through the payment-event core, but two blocking regressions remain: autogenerated auto-settle idempotency/reference values no longer include the resolved allocation set, and the implementation still does not satisfy the mission contract's required due-date-first supplier allocation order.

issues.description: When callers omit both `referenceNumber` and `idempotencyKey`, `autoSettleSupplier` now generates the fallback reference with `buildSupplierAutoSettlementReference(..., List.of())`, so the derived reference/idempotency key depends only on supplier + cash account + amount. Two distinct auto-settle requests for the same supplier and amount but different open-purchase sets will therefore collide and replay the first posting instead of creating a new one. The helper explicitly fingerprints allocations in `SettlementReferenceService.buildSupplierAutoSettlementReference` (see lines 186-217), and the parent implementation built allocations before generating this fallback key. The new E2E only covers the explicit-key path (`ProcureToPayE2ETest.java:750-758`), so this regression is untested.

The feature claims fulfillment of `VAL-PARTNER-016`, but supplier auto-settle still orders candidates only by `invoiceDate, id` (`ORDER BY ... p.invoiceDate, p.id`). The validation contract requires oldest-first ordering by due date when available, then invoice date, then ID (`validation-contract.md:384-389`). `RawMaterialPurchase` also has no due-date field (`RawMaterialPurchase.java:50-66`), so the implementation cannot satisfy that contract, and the new E2E assertion only checks `firstPurchaseId < secondPurchaseId` (`ProcureToPayE2ETest.java:748`) rather than any due-date priority.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared state currently states that supplier auto-settle ordering is due-date-first (`VAL-PARTNER-016`), but the purchasing model exposes only `invoiceDate` and the repository orders only by `invoiceDate, id`. This contract/model mismatch should be triaged explicitly so future workers do not claim proof for a due-date rule the codebase cannot presently express.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:384-389 requires due-date ordering; erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/purchasing/domain/RawMaterialPurchase.java:50-66 has no due-date field; erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/purchasing/domain/RawMaterialPurchaseRepository.java:124-132 orders by invoiceDate/id only.

summary: Fail. The payment-event unification landed, but the feature still has a blocking auto-generated idempotency/reference regression for supplier auto-settle and it does not actually satisfy the mission contract's required due-date-first allocation ordering.
